# Obligatory Java to Python Migration Practices

Use this skill for Java→Python migration tasks.

## Rules

1. Preserve behavior first (migrate/add tests before refactoring).
2. Translate Java classes into small focused Python modules.
3. Replace getters/setters with Pythonic properties only when needed.
4. Use type hints and dataclasses for explicit models.
5. Convert checked-exception flows to clear specific Python exceptions.
6. Prefer Python idioms/stdlib over copied Java patterns.
7. Follow naming conventions (`snake_case`, `PascalCase`).
8. Validate performance-sensitive paths post-migration.
9. Run linting, tests, and static checks per migration batch.
